# HonoCube API Framework Documentation

HonoCube API is a lightweight, declarative resource-based backend framework built on top of Hono and Drizzle ORM. It is designed to rapidly build RESTful APIs with built-in validation, relations, caching, database execution strategies, and rate limiting.

## 1. Setup and Initialization

HonoCube is initialized using `createApp`, providing the global config (adapter, strategy, logger). You then use `defineResource` to create endpoints, and `defineApi` to group them.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono';
import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/libsql';
import { createClient } from '@libsql/client';
import { DrizzleAdapter, createApp } from '@honocube/api';
import * as schema from './schema';

const client = createClient({ url: process.env.DATABASE_URL! });
const db = drizzle(client, { schema });

const adapter = new DrizzleAdapter(db, { dialect: 'sqlite' });

const { defineResource, defineApi } = createApp({
  adapter,
  strategy: 'batch', // "batch" (Cloudflare D1 optimized), "transaction", or "none"
});
```

## 2. Defining Resources

A resource maps a database table to standard REST CRUD endpoints (`GET /`, `GET /:id`, `POST /`, `PATCH /:id`, `DELETE /:id`, `PATCH /batch-update`, `POST /batch-delete`).

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

const usersResource = defineResource({
  name: 'users',
  table: schema.users,
  methods: ['list', 'detail', 'create', 'update', 'delete'], // Defaults to all 5
  timestamps: true, // Auto-manages createdAt and updatedAt
  softDelete: 'deletedAt', // Automatically ignores rows where deletedAt is not null
  pagination: {
    defaultLimit: 20,
    maxLimit: 100 // Hard cap to prevent database overload
  },
  validator: z.object({
    name: z.string(),
    email: z.string().email(),
  }),
  search: {
    fields: ['name', 'email'], // Allows deep subquery searching via ?q=
  },
  filter: {
    allow: ['status', 'role'] // Explicitly allow query param filtering
  },
  defaultSort: { field: 'id', direction: 'desc' }, // Used when no sort param is provided
});
```

## 3. Relationships

HonoCube handles nested writes, relationship queries, and syncing pivot tables automatically.

```typescript
const postsResource = defineResource({
  name: 'posts',
  table: schema.posts,
  validator: z.object({ title: z.string(), userId: z.number() }),
  relations: {
    one: [
      {
        name: 'author',
        table: schema.users,
        foreignKey: 'userId',
        referenceKey: 'id',
        search: ['name', 'email'] // Parent is searchable by relational data
      }
    ],
    many: [
      {
        name: 'comments',
        table: schema.comments,
        foreignKey: 'postId',
        referenceKey: 'id',
        schema: z.object({ text: z.string() })
      }
    ]
  },
  with: { author: true, comments: true } // Return these relations in GET responses
});
```

When creating or updating `posts`, passing a `comments` array will automatically insert or sync the child table (optimizing into a single D1 `batch` call if configured).

## 4. Querying the API (For Frontend/Admin Clients)

When building frontends, understand these URL parameters:

*   **Pagination**: `?limit=20&page=2`
*   **Search**: `?q=searchterm` (Searches `search.fields` and `search.relations` using highly optimized SQL subqueries to bypass SQLite variable limits).
*   **Exact Filter**: `?status=active` (Must be explicitly allowed in `filter.allow`).
*   **Range Filters**: `?price__gte=100&price__lte=500` (Supported suffixes: `__gte`, `__lte`, `__gt`, `__lt`, `__like`).

*Example Frontend Fetch:*
`GET /api/posts?q=hello&status=published&price__gte=10&limit=50&page=1`

## 5. Standard Responses

**Success:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "id": 1, "name": "Item" }, // Object for detail/create/update, Array for list
  "meta": { "total": 100, "page": 1, "limit": 20, "totalPages": 5 } // Only on list
}
```

**Error:**
```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR", // e.g. VALIDATION_ERROR, NOT_FOUND, INTERNAL_ERROR
    "message": "Validation failed",
    "details": [...] // Zod issues if validation fails
  }
}
```

## 6. Batch Operations

HonoCube supports batch updates and deletes.
*   **Batch Update**: `PATCH /batch-update` - Body: `{ "ids": [1,2], "data": { "status": "active" } }`
*   **Batch Delete**: `POST /batch-delete` - Body: `{ "ids": [1,2] }`

## 7. Database Strategy

The `createApp` takes a `strategy` config:
*   `batch` (Default): Compiles multi-statement operations (e.g. parent insert + relational inserts) into Drizzle `db.batch()`. Highly optimized for Cloudflare D1. Utilizes `(SELECT last_insert_rowid())` internally.
*   `transaction`: Uses traditional `db.transaction()` wrapper.
*   `none`: Executes sequentially without wrapping logic.

---

## 8. API Behavioral Mechanics

To accurately consume or extend the HonoCube API, an AI must understand its internal mechanics.

### Search Execution (Subqueries vs. Arrays)
When a user passes `?q=term`, the API **does not** pull matching IDs into memory. Instead, it generates nested SQL `SELECT` statements (subqueries) for the main table and any related tables configured in `search.relations`. These subqueries are wrapped in a raw `sql` template literal to prevent Drizzle ORM from prematurely evaluating them. This ensures the API can securely search through millions of records without hitting Cloudflare D1's 512 variable limit. 
*Constraint:* If you add a custom search override, you MUST use SQL subqueries; do not use `inArray` with a mapped array of IDs.

### Default Sorting
If a client calls `GET /` without a sort parameter, and the backend developer has not provided a `defaultSort` in the `ResourceConfig`, the API will automatically fallback to sorting by `id` descending (`{ field: 'id', direction: 'desc' }`).

### Execution Strategies & Atomicity
*   **`batch`**: In this mode, `create` and `update` requests that include nested relationships do not execute sequential queries. They build an array of statement builders (`adapter.insertStmt`). For related children, the framework uses the raw SQL function `(SELECT last_insert_rowid())` as the foreign key value. All statements are sent to the database in one single `db.batch()` network call.
*   **`transaction`**: Traditional SQL transactions. Should be avoided in D1 if possible.
*   **`none`**: Executes queries sequentially.

### Relational Syncing (The "Replace" Strategy)
When updating a record via `PATCH /:id` and providing a nested array of relations (e.g., `many` or `pivots`), the default relation strategy is `"replace"`. 
This means the API will dynamically execute a `DELETE` query against the child table where the `foreignKey` matches the current `id`, followed immediately by `INSERT` queries for the new array payload. If you are building a frontend, be aware that omitting a child from the array during a `PATCH` request will delete it from the database.

### Pagination Safeguards
Clients use `?page=X&limit=Y`. However, the API enforces a `maxLimit` (defaults to 100). If an AI client attempts to request `?limit=5000`, the API will silently cap the query to 100 rows to protect the database engine.

---

## 9. Core Configuration Types (TypeScript)

For an exact understanding of the configuration surface, refer to these core TypeScript interfaces.

```typescript
export interface AppConfig<Env extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>, AppContext = any> {
  adapter: DatabaseAdapter<any, any, any> | ((c: Context<{ Bindings: Env }>) => DatabaseAdapter<any, any, any>);
  strategy?: "batch" | "transaction" | "none"; 
  middleware?: MiddlewareHandler<{ Bindings: Env }>[];
  schema?: Record<string, any>;
  logger?: AppLogger;
  cache?: AppCache;
  rateLimiter?: RateLimiter;
  storage?: AppStorage;
  mediaBaseUrl?: string;
  onEvent?: (event: ResourceEvent<AppContext>) => Promise<void> | void;
  getContext?: (c: Context<{ Bindings: Env }>) => Promise<AppContext> | AppContext;
  auth?: {
    can: (appContext: AppContext, permission: string, record?: any) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;
  };
}

export interface ResourceConfig<TTable = unknown, TSelect = unknown, TInsert = unknown, Env extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>, AppContext = any> {
  name: string;
  queryKey?: string;
  table: TTable;
  schema?: Record<string, any>;
  adapter?: DatabaseAdapter<TTable, TSelect, TInsert> | ((c: Context<{ Bindings: Env }>) => DatabaseAdapter<TTable, TSelect, TInsert>);
  
  validator: 
    | z.ZodType<TInsert, any, any> 
    | [z.ZodType<TInsert, any, any>, z.ZodTypeAny]
    | { create: z.ZodType<TInsert, any, any>; update: z.ZodTypeAny };
  
  middleware?: MiddlewareHandler<{ Bindings: Env }>[];
  methods?: ("list" | "detail" | "create" | "update" | "delete" | "batch-update" | "batch-delete")[];
  softDelete?: string; 
  
  permissions?: {
    list?: string; detail?: string; create?: string; update?: string; delete?: string;
    "batch-update"?: string; "batch-delete"?: string;
  };
  authorize?: (appContext: AppContext, action: "list" | "detail" | "create" | "update" | "delete" | "batch-update" | "batch-delete", record?: TSelect) => boolean | Promise<boolean>;

  hidden?: (keyof TSelect)[];
  transform?: (record: TSelect, appContext: AppContext) => any | Promise<any>;
  
  cache?: { enabled: boolean; ttl?: number; };
  rateLimit?: { limit: number; window: string; key?: (c: Context<{ Bindings: Env }>, appContext: AppContext) => string; };
  uploads?: Record<string, { folder?: string }>;
  timestamps?: boolean | { createdAt?: string; updatedAt?: string; };
  scope?: (cols: any, ops: any, appContext: AppContext) => any;

  search?: { fields?: (keyof TSelect | string)[]; relations?: string[]; };
  filter?: { allow?: (keyof TSelect | string)[]; };
  defaultSort?: { field: string; direction: "asc" | "desc" };
  pagination?: { defaultLimit?: number; maxLimit?: number };
  
  relations?: {
    one?: OneConfig<any>[];
    pivots?: PivotConfig<any>[];
    many?: ManyConfig<any>[];
  };

  with?: Record<string, any>;
  hooks?: ResourceHooks<TSelect, TInsert, Env>;
  actions?: ActionConfig<Env, AppContext>[];
}

export interface OneConfig<TTable = any> {
  name: string; 
  table: TTable;
  foreignKey: string; 
  referenceKey: string; 
  search?: string[]; 
  hidden?: string[]; 
}

export interface PivotConfig<TTable = any> {
  name: string;
  table: TTable;
  targetTable?: any; 
  foreignKey: string; 
  referenceKey: string; 
  targetReferenceKey: string; 
  relationName: string;
  strategy?: "upsert" | "replace" | "append";
  search?: string[]; 
  hidden?: string[]; 
}

export interface ManyConfig<TTable = any> {
  name: string;
  table: TTable;
  foreignKey: string; 
  referenceKey: string; 
  schema: z.ZodSchema;
  strategy?: "upsert" | "replace" | "append";
  search?: string[]; 
  hidden?: string[]; 
}

export interface ResourceHooks<TSelect = unknown, TInsert = unknown, Env = any, AppContext = any> {
  beforeFetch?: (c: Context, params: { list?: boolean; detailId?: string | number }, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<void> | void;
  afterFetch?: (rows: TSelect[], c: Context, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<TSelect[]> | TSelect[];
  beforeCreate?: (data: TInsert, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<TInsert | void> | TInsert | void;
  afterCreate?: (record: TSelect, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<void> | void;
  beforeUpdate?: (id: string | number, data: Partial<TInsert>, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<Partial<TInsert> | void> | Partial<TInsert> | void;
  afterUpdate?: (record: TSelect, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<void> | void;
  beforeDelete?: (id: string | number, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<void> | void;
  afterDelete?: (id: string | number, c: Context, tx: any, appContext: AppContext) => Promise<void> | void;
  // Batch Hooks also available...
}
```
